segments.text: He arose from there and came into the borders of Judea and beyond the Jordan. Multitudes came together to him again. As he usually did, he was again teaching them.
Pharisees came to him testing him, and asked him,
Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: He answered,
What did Moses command you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They said,
Moses allowed a certificate of divorce to be written, and to divorce her.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: But Jesus said to them,
For your hardness of heart, he wrote you this commandment.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But from the beginning of the creation, God made them male and female.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For this cause a man will leave his father and mother, and will join to his wife,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and the two will become one flesh, so that they are no longer two, but one flesh.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What therefore God has joined together, let no man separate.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the house, his disciples asked him again about the same matter.
He said to them,
Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If a woman herself divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They were bringing to him little children, that he should touch them, but the disciples rebuked those who were bringing them.
But when Jesus saw it, he was moved with indignation and said to them,
Allow the little children to come to me! Don’t forbid them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Most certainly I tell you, whoever will not receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He took them in his arms and blessed them, laying his hands on them.
As he was going out into the way, one ran to him, knelt before him, and asked him,
Good Teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
Why do you call me good? No one is good except one—God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You know the commandments: ‘Do not murder,’ ‘Do not commit adultery,’ ‘Do not steal,’ ‘Do not give false testimony,’ ‘Do not defraud,’ ‘Honor your father and mother.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to him,
Teacher, I have observed all these things from my youth.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus looking at him loved him, and said to him,
One thing you lack. Go, sell whatever you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me, taking up the cross.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But his face fell at that saying, and he went away sorrowful, for he was one who had great possessions.
Jesus looked around and said to his disciples,
How difficult it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answered again,
Children, how hard it is for those who trust in riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They were exceedingly astonished, saying to him,
Then who can be saved?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus, looking at them, said,
With men it is impossible, but not with God, for all things are possible with God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Peter began to tell him,
Behold, we have left all and have followed you.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Jesus said,
Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or land, for my sake, and for the sake of the Good News,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but he will receive one hundred times more now in this time: houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and land, with persecutions; and in the age to come eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They were on the way, going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus was going in front of them, and they were amazed; and those who followed were afraid. He again took the twelve, and began to tell them the things that were going to happen to him.
Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and the scribes. They will condemn him to death, and will deliver him to the Gentiles.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They will mock him, spit on him, scourge him, and kill him. On the third day he will rise again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came near to him, saying,
Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we will ask.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to them,
What do you want me to do for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They said to him,
Grant to us that we may sit, one at your right hand and one at your left hand, in your glory.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But Jesus said to them,
You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They said to him,
We are able.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to them,
You shall indeed drink the cup that I drink, and you shall be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but to sit at my right hand and at my left hand is not mine to give, but for whom it has been prepared.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the ten heard it, they began to be indignant toward James and John.
Jesus summoned them and said to them,
You know that they who are recognized as rulers over the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But it shall not be so among you, but whoever wants to become great among you shall be your servant.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever of you wants to become first among you shall be bondservant of all.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For the Son of Man also came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They came to Jericho. As he went out from Jericho with his disciples and a great multitude, the son of Timaeus, Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the road.
When he heard that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out and say,
Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many rebuked him, that he should be quiet, but he cried out much more,
You son of David, have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Many

segments.text: Jesus stood still and said,
Call him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They called the blind man, saying to him,
Cheer up! Get up. He is calling you!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He, casting away his cloak, sprang up, and came to Jesus.
Jesus asked him,
What do you want me to do for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The blind man said to him,
Rabboni, [^23] that I may see again.
segments.speaker: The blind man

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
Go your way. Your faith has made you well.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus on the way.
